requirements_for_qualification.summary: Must exchange qualifying real property held for business or investment purposes for like-kind real property to defer capital gain recognition and preserve investment basis through qualified intermediary facilitated exchange
criteria: After TCJA, §1031 exchanges limited to real property only; personal property no longer qualifies
Both relinquished and replacement property must be held for productive use in business or for investment
Properties must be like-kind - generally, all U.S. real estate considered like-kind regardless of type or quality
Must use qualified intermediary to facilitate exchange; cannot take direct receipt of proceeds
45-day identification period to identify potential replacement properties (strict deadline, no extensions)
180-day exchange period to complete acquisition of replacement property from identification date
Can identify up to 3 properties regardless of value, OR unlimited properties if total value ≤200% relinquished property
Boot (cash or non-like-kind property) received triggers recognized gain to extent of boot received

section_1031_requirements.gain_deferral: Defers capital gain and depreciation recapture; does not eliminate tax but postpones recognition
section_1031_requirements.basis_carryover: Replacement property takes carryover basis adjusted for gain recognized and boot received
section_1031_requirements.depreciation_preservation: Can continue depreciation schedule on replacement property based on carryover basis
section_1031_requirements.real_property_only: Post-TCJA limited to real property; no longer available for equipment, vehicles, personal property
section_1031_requirements.qualified_intermediary: Must use QI to avoid constructive receipt; direct exchange or receipt of proceeds disqualifies
section_1031_requirements.strict_deadlines: 45-day identification and 180-day completion deadlines strictly enforced; no extensions allowed

restrictions: Primary residence, property held primarily for sale, and inventory do not qualify for §1031 treatment
Related party exchanges subject to 2-year holding period to prevent basis step-up planning abuse
Cannot exchange U.S. real property for foreign real property; must be like-kind and same jurisdiction
Debt structure must be maintained or increased; debt reduction treated as boot triggering gain recognition
Reverse exchanges (acquire replacement before selling relinquished) require Exchange Accommodation Titleholder
Improvement/construction exchanges complex; must complete improvements within 180-day period
State tax treatment varies; some states don't recognize §1031 exchanges requiring state gain recognition
Estate planning consideration: stepped-up basis at death eliminates deferred gain creating permanent exclusion
